---
title: "Trusted Evidence"
subtitle: "in the era of misinformation"
---

## I am here

-   To spot misinformation \vspace{.5cm}

-   To find what works in healthcare \vspace{.5cm}

-   To produce trusted evidence \vspace{.5cm}

## The scandal of medical research

![The landscape of published systematic reviews](images/epidemic.jpg){fig-align="center" width="500"}

## The infodemic

Misinformation has become an epidemic

We must use explicit, planned methods to identify, appraise, & summarize empirical research to answer specific questions

1.  Prevalence
2.  Etiology
3.  Diagnosis
4.  Therapy
5.  Prognosis

## Evidence to Decision

![Evidence to Decision Framework](images/research2care.png){fig-align="center" width="528"}

# The non-sense

## Mozzarella for PhD

![Mozzarella intake per capita for improving outcomes](images/cheese.jpg){fig-align="center" width="310" height="260"}

## **The Parachute enigma**

![Parachute use to prevent death and major trauma related to gravitational challenge: systematic review of randomized controlled trials](images/parachute.jpg){fig-align="center" width="466"}

## The Ivermectin hoax

-   Fabricated RCTS

-   Flawed systematic review

## Clinical vs statistical significance

![](images/clin_not_stat.jpg)

## Real Clinicians make clinical decisions

::::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}
\vspace{.5cm}

-   The size of a treatment effect \vspace{.5cm}

-   The severity of the condition \vspace{.5cm}

-   The balance between benefits and harms
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/rip.jpg){width="200" height="225"}
:::
:::::

## Process of producing trusted evidence

![From inception to implementation](images/steps.jpg){fig-align="center"}

## **See you next week**

> **Cheers**
